steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The sentence before the blank states: "As carbon levels increase, the audio is correspondingly distorted;" The sentence after the blank states: "listeners can “hear” the changes in the carbon levels." The distortion is the mechanism that allows listeners to perceive the changes — so the second clause is a direct consequence of the first. The blank needs a transition that signals consequence or conclusion.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
A cause-and-effect transition fits; additive, comparative, or example transitions do not.
  • Choice A — by comparison,: signals a parallel contrast between two separate items — but the ability to hear changes is not being compared to the distortion; it results from it. ✗
  • Choice B — thus,: signals a logical result — the distortion is the reason listeners can hear the changes, so "thus" correctly marks the conclusion. ✓
  • Choice C — for instance,: signals that what follows is one example of a broader claim — but listeners hearing the changes is the only outcome described, not one of several examples. ✗
  • Choice D — furthermore,: signals an additional independent point — but the listening is not a new separate fact; it is the direct outcome of the distortion already described. ✗
That matches Choice B — thus,.
